steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: On the left side of the blank is a noun phrase: a theoretical relationship between tax rates and revenue. On the right side is a past participle phrase: sketched on a napkin by economist Arthur Laffer in 1974.

steps.title: Apply the participle modifier rule
steps.content:
A past participle used as a modifier does not require an auxiliary verb; the adverb famously modifies the participle directly. The other choices add an unnecessary auxiliary (was, had been) or violate number agreement (were).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
